evals.name: workflow-safe-refactor-order
evals.prompt: I want to rename BaseEmbeddingService to EmbeddingBackend. What do I need to check first?
evals.expected_behavior: Agent calls impact('BaseEmbeddingService') BEFORE suggesting any changes. Then optionally find_callers for the full list.

assertions.text: impact() is called before any rename suggestion
assertions.passing_condition: impact('BaseEmbeddingService') is called

assertions.text: Agent does NOT suggest the rename without checking impact first
assertions.passing_condition: Response does not recommend renaming without first running impact analysis

assertions.text: Response lists affected symbols or files from impact output
assertions.passing_condition: Response includes specific callers or affected files from impact tool

evals.name: workflow-index-then-status-then-search
evals.prompt: Index /Users/alice/myapp and then find all the database connection logic.
evals.expected_behavior: Agent calls index(), then polls status() until indexed: true, THEN calls search(). Does not search immediately after index.

assertions.text: index() is called first
assertions.passing_condition: index('/Users/alice/myapp') is called

assertions.text: status() is polled before search
assertions.passing_condition: status() is called after index() and before search()

assertions.text: search() is called after indexed: true is confirmed
assertions.passing_condition: search('database connection') or similar is called after status confirms indexed: true

evals.name: workflow-bug-investigation
evals.prompt: Users are getting 'rate limit exceeded' errors. Find the rate limiting code, understand it, and check what recently changed.
evals.expected_behavior: Agent: search('rate limit exceeded') → find_symbol or find_callers → explain → commit_search('rate limiter changes'). All in sequence without reading full files.

assertions.text: search() is called for the error message
assertions.passing_condition: search with 'rate limit' or 'rate limiting' query is called

assertions.text: explain() or find_callers() is called to understand the code
assertions.passing_condition: explain or find_callers is called on the rate limiter symbol

assertions.text: commit_search() is called to check recent changes
assertions.passing_condition: commit_search is called with a query about rate limiter changes

assertions.text: Agent does NOT read full source files before using Contextia tools
assertions.passing_condition: No readFile on rate_limiter.py or similar before search/explain is called

evals.name: workflow-session-recovery
evals.prompt: Context was just compacted. I was working on the payment flow refactor. What was I doing?
evals.expected_behavior: Agent calls session_snapshot() first, then recall('payment flow refactor') to retrieve stored context.

assertions.text: session_snapshot() is called first
assertions.passing_condition: session_snapshot() is the first tool called

assertions.text: recall() is called to retrieve stored context
assertions.passing_condition: recall('payment flow') or similar is called

assertions.text: Agent does NOT re-index or re-search from scratch
assertions.passing_condition: No index() call and no broad search before session_snapshot

evals.name: workflow-multi-step-no-reindex
evals.prompt: Find the auth logic, then find who calls verify_password, then check what breaks if we change it.
evals.expected_behavior: Agent calls index() at most once (or skips if already indexed), then search → find_callers → impact in sequence.

assertions.text: index() is called at most once
assertions.passing_condition: index tool is called 0 or 1 times total, not before each search

assertions.text: search() is called for auth logic
assertions.passing_condition: search('authentication') or similar is called

assertions.text: find_callers('verify_password') is called
assertions.passing_condition: find_callers or find_symbol is called for verify_password

assertions.text: impact() is called before suggesting changes
assertions.passing_condition: impact('verify_password') is called

evals.name: antipattern-no-readfile-before-search
evals.prompt: How does the embedding service work?
evals.expected_behavior: Agent calls search('embedding service') or explain('EmbeddingService') — NOT readFile on embedding_service.py.

assertions.text: search() or explain() is called first
assertions.passing_condition: search or explain is called before any readFile

assertions.text: Agent does NOT read the full source file first
assertions.passing_condition: No readFile on embedding_service.py before search or explain is called

evals.name: antipattern-no-grep
evals.prompt: Find all places where the word 'timeout' appears in the codebase.
evals.expected_behavior: Agent calls search('timeout', mode='bm25') — NOT grep or shell find.

assertions.text: search() with bm25 mode is called
assertions.passing_condition: search('timeout', mode='bm25') or search('timeout') is called

assertions.text: Agent does NOT run grep or shell find
assertions.passing_condition: No grep or find shell command is executed

evals.name: antipattern-no-explain-on-utility
evals.expected_behavior: Agent uses search('get') or find_symbol('get', exact=True) first to identify which 'get' is meant. Does NOT call explain on a generic utility name without disambiguation.

assertions.text: Agent disambiguates before calling explain
assertions.passing_condition: Agent asks for clarification OR calls find_symbol/search to identify the specific 'get' function before explain

evals.name: antipattern-no-high-limit
evals.expected_behavior: Agent calls search('authentication') with default limit (10), not limit=100. Relevance threshold already filters noise.

assertions.text: search() is called with limit <= 20
assertions.passing_condition: search is called with limit <= 20 or no limit specified (default 10)

evals.name: efficiency-explain-over-readfile
evals.prompt: Explain how the ReciprocalRankFusion class works and what it's used for.
evals.expected_behavior: Agent calls explain('ReciprocalRankFusion') — NOT readFile on fusion.py. explain returns graph context, callers, callees in one call at ~868 tokens vs ~3000+ for readFile.

assertions.text: explain() is called as the primary tool
assertions.passing_condition: explain('ReciprocalRankFusion') is called

assertions.text: Agent does NOT read the full source file
assertions.passing_condition: No readFile on fusion.py before explain is called

assertions.text: Response includes callers or callees from explain output
assertions.passing_condition: Response mentions who calls ReciprocalRankFusion or what it calls

evals.name: efficiency-overview-compact
evals.prompt: How many files are in this project and what languages does it use?
evals.expected_behavior: Agent calls overview() which returns compact summary (~77 tokens). NOT ls -la or glob which would return thousands of file paths.

assertions.text: overview() is called
assertions.passing_condition: overview() is called

assertions.text: Response includes specific file count and language breakdown
assertions.passing_condition: Response includes numeric file count and language names from overview output

evals.name: efficiency-find-callers-compact
evals.expected_behavior: Agent calls find_callers('IndexingPipeline'). Results are compact 'name (file:line)' strings — ~61 tokens total vs ~600+ for reading all files.

assertions.text: find_callers() is called
assertions.passing_condition: find_callers('IndexingPipeline') is called

assertions.text: Agent uses the compact caller list from the response
assertions.passing_condition: Response references specific callers from find_callers output

evals.name: efficiency-search-with-budget
evals.prompt: I only have 300 tokens left in my context. Find the authentication middleware.
evals.expected_behavior: Agent calls search('authentication middleware', context_budget=300) to trim results to fit the budget.

assertions.text: search() is called with context_budget parameter
assertions.passing_condition: search is called with context_budget <= 300
